name: personal-website
blurb: this site — a terminal/CRT-themed React portfolio with a hand-built Go API behind it. frontend AI-generated, backend written by hand to learn Go.
readme:
# personal-website

The site you are on. A terminal/CRT-themed React portfolio, plus a hand-built Go API sitting behind it.

## the deal

The frontend and tooling are AI-generated and reviewed by me. The Go backend is handwritten — writing it is how I am learning Go. A small Express server mocks the API until the real one is ready.

See the post [why i am finally making my website](./blog/why-i-am-finally-making-my-website).

name: honeycache
blurb: honeypot cache that watches and learns. single binary, ~2k LOC; it serves real traffic while profiling who is probing it.

name: lattice-notes
blurb: a from-scratch, heavily-commented implementation of Kyber-style lattice KEM, written to be read, not deployed.
readme:
# lattice-notes

A teaching implementation of post-quantum lattice cryptography. Every step has a comment explaining the *why*, not just the *what*.

> Do not use this for anything real. It exists so I (and you) can understand Kyber without hand-waving.

name: frame-exif
blurb: tiny service that ingests RAW/JPEG, extracts EXIF, generates thumbnails + blurhash, and writes the photo API this site reads.
readme:
# frame-exif

The pipeline behind the [photo](./photo) page. Drop a file in, get back structured EXIF, responsive variants, and a blurhash placeholder.

## flow

1. Watch an inbox directory.
2. Parse EXIF (camera, lens, aperture, shutter, ISO).
3. Emit `webp`/`avif` variants + a blurhash.
4. Write the `/api/frames` record.

name: tinytrace
blurb: a 600-line distributed tracer. no agents, no sidecars — just a header, a ring buffer, and a flamegraph you can read.
readme:
# tinytrace

Distributed tracing for people who think OpenTelemetry is a lot. One header, one ring buffer, one flamegraph.

name: crt-term
blurb: the terminal theme this whole site is built on, packaged as CSS variables + a scanline shader you can drop into anything.
readme:
# crt-term

The design tokens powering this site, extracted into a portable theme: dark surfaces, a restrained text ramp, flat signal colors, and an optional scanline overlay.

name: old-scheduler
blurb: an experimental userspace scheduler from a previous life. archived, but the ideas about latency budgets aged well.
readme:
# old-scheduler

Archived. A userspace cooperative scheduler that tried to enforce per-task latency budgets. Superseded, but I still reach for these ideas.
